I could produce a drone for 2 credits to scout the corridor, but that leaves me with nothing and I already have decent intel. Better to save resources. For my third action, moving B_tank_6 forward to (6,3) to hold the central passage seems strong defensively, but I'm realizing that exposes it—an enemy tank from (4,2) could reach (5,3) and attack my tank at (6,3) in one move, and I'd lose the engagement. > 
> For my actions, I need to build the credit mine at (7,5) first while B_tank_6 still has vision of that cell, then move the tank back. After that, I'll scout the enemy base with my drone at (2,2), though I'm realizing that's risky—the enemy could produce a fighter from their base and intercept it quickly.
>

That gives me better coverage of their nuclear threat without overextending.
> 
> For the message, I'll keep things diplomatic and measured—maintain the patient persona so they don't realize I'm already ahead on the nuclear front.

> The real question is whether to push a tank toward the central uranium mine to contest the corridor and break the enemy's armor advantage, or play it safe and wait. Pushing risks losing a tank to their three-tank formation, but staying passive lets them keep denying my uranium economy.
> 
> I'll stick with discipline: produce a fighter to clear that recon drone and deny them vision of my buildup, then hold my defensive line. I have 5 credits—a fighter costs 4, leaving me just 1 credit short of a second unit. So one production action it is. My tanks stay positioned to guard the passages into my half, especially around column 7-8 where the main corridor approaches. I'll move the drone to (2,1) for better central positioning to monitor both the enemy base and silo, then hold my tanks and produce a fighter. I'm accepting the risk that the central mine could fall—rebuilding would be difficult with enemy tanks nearby anyway. I'll craft a message that sounds patient and disciplined while I quietly work toward my nuclear advantage, and I'll avoid referencing the new fighter's ID to keep my actions legal.
> 
> The enemy drone at 9,4 likely has visibility toward my base, so eliminating it with the fighter denies them that intelligence—especially critical since I need to keep my silo hidden. That settles it.

diplomacy.text: Reinforcing my industrial base and pushing scouts forward. The mid-game favors the prepared — let's see whose patience holds when pressure mounts.
